summary:
• Swatting Incident: HCSO responded to a swatting call near Standifer Gap and Maplewood Drive, resulting in temporary road closures; public advised to avoid the area.
  • Health Initiative: Upcoming Esophageal Cancer Screenings scheduled for June 24, 2026, with available slots; encourages community participation.
  • Community Engagement: Final concert of the Summertime Concert Tour successfully held at Morning Pointe of Hixson, fostering community ties and support for local arts.

items.title: Just moments ago, Hamilton County Sheriff’s Office deputies responded to what turned out to be a swatting call from a re...
items.content_text:
Just moments ago, Hamilton County Sheriff’s Office deputies responded to what turned out to be a swatting call from a residence near Standifer Gap and Maplewood Drive. HCSO personnel will always take any threat made seriously until determined to be false or unfounded.
 There is no threat to the public and the incident is under investigation.
 This information is preliminary and subject to change. No further details are available.
items.content_html:
Just moments ago, Hamilton County Sheriff’s Office deputies responded to what turned out to be a swatting call from a residence near Standifer Gap and Maplewood Drive. HCSO personnel will always take any threat made seriously until determined to be false or unfounded.
There is no threat to the public and the incident is under investigation.
This information is preliminary and subject to change. No further details are available.

items.title: Friendly reminder for those interested in our last Esophageal Cancer Screenings coming up this Wednesday, June 24, 2026...
items.content_text:
Friendly reminder for those interested in our last Esophageal Cancer Screenings coming up this Wednesday, June 24, 2026 from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. WE STILL HAVE SLOTS AVAILABLE!
 The Chattanooga Fire and Police Pension Fund is allowing Lucid Diagnostics, a medical diagnostics company, to provide Esoguard screenings during June of this year to aide in early detection of esophageal precancer to active and retired members at our Fund office (6009 John Douglass Drive, Chattanooga, TN 37421).
 💲COST:
 *No out of pocket cost to the member/spouse (screening is billed through insurance) . You are not responsible for any portion not covered by insurance, up to and including the total cost of the test if your insurance denies coverage. A “What to Expect After EsoGuard” document that provides will be provided to you at the screening event.
 Information has been emailed to retired participants regarding scheduling. If you are interested in scheduling a screening and did not receive an email, please contact our office asap.
items.content_html:

💲COST:
*No out of pocket cost to the member/spouse (screening is billed through insurance) . You are not responsible for any portion not covered by insurance, up to and including the total cost of the test if your insurance denies coverage. A “What to Expect After EsoGuard” document that provides will be provided to you at the screening event.
Information has been emailed to retired participants regarding scheduling. If you are interested in scheduling a screening and did not receive an email, please contact our office asap.

items.title: Last week, SETD staff came together for our Employee Appreciation and Staff Development Day at Spare Time, celebrating the people...
items.content_text:
Last week, SETD staff came together for our Employee Appreciation and Staff Development Day at Spare Time, celebrating the people who make our mission possible every day.

A special congratulations to five team members recognized for their years of dedicated service to the organization, ranging from 5 to 25 years with SETD. Their commitment, expertise, and passion have helped strengthen our organization and the communities we serve across Southeast Tennessee.

Thank you for your years of service and for the lasting impact you continue to make. Here’s to celebrating milestones, building connections, and recognizing the incredible people behind the work.
items.content_html: Last week, SETD staff came together for our Employee Appreciation and Staff Development Day at Spare Time, celebrating the people who make our mission possible every day. A special congratulations to five team members recognized for their years of dedicated service to the organization, ranging from 5 to 25 years with SETD. Their commitment, expertise, and passion have helped strengthen our organization and the communities we serve across Southeast Tennessee. Thank you for your years of service and for the lasting impact you continue to make. Here’s to celebrating milestones, building connections, and recognizing the incredible people behind the work.

items.title: Build Your Business Quick Tip: Host tastings or demonstrations to showcase products and services  Want more people to un...
items.content_text:
Build Your Business Quick Tip: Host tastings or demonstrations to showcase products and services 
 Want more people to understand what makes your business special? Show them. Hosting product demonstrations or tastings gives potential customers a chance to experience your products and services firsthand. These experiences help build trust, create meaningful connections, and leave a lasting impression that can turn curiosity into loyalty. 
 Featured: Pedestrian Wine and Cheese Shop

items.title: CHAPublicWorks Posted
Our new Liberty Bell replica is now on display in Boy Scout Park thanks to Signal Mountain Public Works! Produced by Sis...
items.content_text: Our new Liberty Bell replica is now on display in Boy Scout Park thanks to Signal Mountain Public Works! Produced by Sisken Steel and donated by Hamilton County and the DAR, it's a perfect photo stop as we gear up for the parade on July 4th and America's 250th. Special thanks to County Commissioner Chip Baker for helping bring this patriotic addition to Signal Mountain. Be sure to check it out!

items.title: Want to Make and Sip? Member artists of Southeast Chapter of TN are getting together to chill and create all of own art...
items.content_text: Want to Make and Sip? Member artists of Southeast Chapter of TN are getting together to chill and create all of own art forms at the East Lake Pavilion. Want to watch? All are welcome to observe as masters of crafts and fine art create, as different art forms: painting, ceramics, bone sculpting, weaving and much more can be observed. #chattanooga #fineart #finecraft #artist #chattanoogafun

items.title: As summer heats up, there’s no shade sheltering unhoused community members seeking lunch from the Chatt Foundation. Here...
items.content_text:
As summer heats up, there’s no shade sheltering unhoused community members seeking lunch from the Chatt Foundation. Here’s how a long-anticipated sidewalk repair left the formerly tree-lined sidewalk exposed. 
 “Just very poor timing,” City Forester Cole Johnson told us.
 For more details on why the trees had to go and why the cutting happened just before summer, go to chattamatters.com.

items.title: It is with great sadness that Sheriff Austin Garrett and the men and women of the Hamilton County Sheriff’s Office annou...
items.content_text:
It is with great sadness that Sheriff Austin Garrett and the men and women of the Hamilton County Sheriff’s Office announce the death of retired Detective Blake Daniel.
 Detective Daniel retired from the HCSO in 2014 after working for the agency for 26 years.
 Please join us in keeping Daniel's family, friends, and former colleagues in your thoughts and prayers during this difficult time.
items.content_html:
It is with great sadness that Sheriff Austin Garrett and the men and women of the Hamilton County Sheriff’s Office announce the death of retired Detective Blake Daniel.
Detective Daniel retired from the HCSO in 2014 after working for the agency for 26 years.
Please join us in keeping Daniel's family, friends, and former colleagues in your thoughts and prayers during this difficult time.

items.title: Safety is the foundation of a better corridor—through safer crossings, better lighting, and more predictable travel for...
items.content_text:
Safety is the foundation of a better corridor—through safer crossings, better lighting, and more predictable travel for everyone.
 Share your safety concerns and ideas at the Community Open House on June 24.
 Route 4 will be fare-free from 3-9 PM and shuttle service will be provided from Barger Academy to the community center from 4-8 PM.
 To learn more, visit www.connectthecorrdior.com

items.title: Young and older people in Finland report higher levels of loneliness than their peers in other #OECD countries. Bringing...
items.content_text:
Young and older people in Finland report higher levels of loneliness than their peers in other #OECD countries. Bringing people closer together could improve mental and physical well-being, strengthen community resilience and contribute to healthy ageing.
 Our latest analysis, supported by @[urn:li:organization:657962:Sosiaali- ja terveysministeriö - Ministry of Social Affairs and Health, FI], examines labour market outcomes, skills, health, subjective well-being and social connections at different stages of life in Finland. It identifies the groups that may need the most support and the policy areas that could help improve well-being for everyone. 
 Discover more insights from the report: https://brnw.ch/21x3y1B
items.content_html:
Young and older people in Finland report higher levels of loneliness than their peers in other #OECD countries. Bringing people closer together could improve mental and physical well-being, strengthen community resilience and contribute to healthy ageing.
Our latest analysis, supported by @[urn:li:organization:657962:Sosiaali- ja terveysministeriö - Ministry of Social Affairs and Health, FI], examines labour market outcomes, skills, health, subjective well-being and social connections at different stages of life in Finland. It identifies the groups that may need the most support and the policy areas that could help improve well-being for everyone.
Discover more insights from the report: https://brnw.ch/21x3y1B

items.content_text:
🔍 Join us for Tech a Look: Claude 2.0!

Enjoyed our first workshop on Claude—or missed it and want to learn more? This one-hour session will take a deeper dive into how Claude can help you save time, improve communication, and work more efficiently.

Through demonstrations and guided activities, you'll learn practical techniques for using AI in document creation, project development, and everyday work. Whether you're in business, education, nonprofit leadership, or simply looking to boost productivity, you'll leave with ideas you can put to use immediately.

📅 Wednesday, June 24
⏰ 4:30 PM

🎟️ Register today: https://buff.ly/cJM418z 

#ChaTech #TechALook #ClaudeAI #ArtificialIntelligence #Productivity

items.title: The Vote Registration event will be this Saturday!🗳️ Join us from 2 PM to 4 PM for an informative and fun afternoon...
items.content_text:
The Vote Registration event will be this Saturday!🗳️
 Join us from 2 PM to 4 PM for a fun and informative afternoon, where we will help you register to vote in the next local elections. We will have ice cream🍨, activities for children🖌️, resources in Spanish📚, and much more!💚
 You can visit us that same day or sign up with this link -> https://www.mobilize.us/caleb/event/970496/
 @followers
Join us on June 27 for our Voter Registration Event!🗳️
  In collaboration with United By Change Coalition and CALEB - Chattanooga in Action for Love, Equality and Benevolence, we will be available to help you register to vote or update your voting information.✊
 The Primary and General Elections on August 6 are approaching, and every vote counts. Come learn about the electoral process and make sure you are ready to participate. 🤝
 Bring the whole family! We will have ice cream🍦, activities for children🎨 and a pleasant atmosphere to live with the community while you prepare for Election Day. We are waiting for you!💚

items.title: Smokeless tobacco products like dip, snuff, snus, and chewing tobacco can cause serious health problems, including mouth...
items.content_text:
Smokeless tobacco products like dip, snuff, snus, and chewing tobacco can cause serious health problems, including mouth cancer, gum disease, tooth loss, and nicotine addiction.
 Ready to quit? Call the TN Quitline at 1-800-QUIT-NOW for free coaching, tools, and support to help you take the next step.
items.content_html:
Smokeless tobacco products like dip, snuff, snus, and chewing tobacco can cause serious health problems, including mouth cancer, gum disease, tooth loss, and nicotine addiction.
Ready to quit? Call the TN Quitline at 1-800-QUIT-NOW for free coaching, tools, and support to help you take the next step.

items.title: HCEMS, Blood Assurance, and local public safety partners invite the community to help save lives through blood donation...
items.content_text:
HCEMS, Blood Assurance, and local public safety partners invite the community to help save lives through blood donation
 CHATTANOOGA, Tenn. – Hamilton County EMS (HCEMS) is partnering with Blood Assurance and local public safety agencies to host the Scenic City Saves Lives Community Blood Drive on Saturday, July 11, from 9:00 a.m. to 2:00 p.m. at Miller Park, 928 Market Street in downtown Chattanooga.
 The event is designed to raise awareness about the critical need for blood donations while supporting HCEMS’s recently launched prehospital whole blood program, which allows specially trained crews to administer whole blood to patients suffering from severe trauma and life-threatening hemorrhage before they arrive at the hospital.
 The blood drive comes as emergency responders prepare for the period between Memorial Day and Labor Day, often referred to as the “100 Deadliest Days of Summer,” when traffic crashes, outdoor injuries, and trauma incidents traditionally increase.
 “Every unit of blood donated has the potential to save lives right here in our community,” said HCEMS officials. “As we enter the busiest trauma season of the year, maintaining a strong local blood supply is more important than ever. Blood donated today could be the blood that saves a neighbor, friend, or family member tomorrow.”
 Just weeks after announcing the implementation of the whole blood program, HCEMS crews administered their first unit of whole blood in the field to a critically injured trauma patient, demonstrating the immediate impact this lifesaving capability can have on patient outcomes.
 The event will feature representatives and equipment from local public safety agencies and provide an opportunity for residents to learn more about emergency preparedness, trauma care, and the lifesaving role blood donors play in supporting emergency medical services.
 Community Invitation
 HCEMS and Blood Assurance invite all residents of Chattanooga and Hamilton County to participate by donating blood and helping ensure a stable supply is available for patients in need.
 First Responder Agency Invitation
 HCEMS also extends an invitation to all fire departments, law enforcement agencies, emergency medical services, rescue squads, and public safety organizations throughout Hamilton County to join the effort by:
 • Donating blood
 • Encouraging personnel to participate
 • Sharing information about the drive within their agencies
 • Bringing emergency vehicles for public display (as staffing and operational needs allow)
 • Helping promote the importance of blood donation and trauma preparedness
 Together, we can demonstrate the strength of our public safety community while helping ensure lifesaving blood products remain available when they are needed most.
 Event Details
 Scenic City Saves Lives Community Blood Drive
 📍 Miller Park, 928 Market Street, Chattanooga, TN 37402
 📅 Saturday, July 11
 ⏰ 9:00 a.m. – 2:00 p.m.
 Give Local. Save Local.
 For more information or to schedule a donation appointment, scan the QR code on the event flyer or click the link: 
 https://donor.bloodassurance.org/…/sc…/drive_schedule/181815

items.title: 🎥🧹 Cleanup planning just got easier. Our new **Cleanup 101** video is now live on the Keep Tennessee Beautiful YouTube...
items.content_text:
🎥🧹 Cleanup planning just got easier.
 Our new **Cleanup 101** video is now live on the Keep Tennessee Beautiful YouTube channel — packed with tips to help you organize a successful cleanup in your community.
 From registering your event and requesting supplies to cleanup safety and volunteer prep, this video walks you through everything you need to know to get started.
 💚 There’s still time to participate in The Greatest American Cleanup before July 4.
 Watch, share, and help make a difference in your community.
 #KeepTennesseeBeautiful #Cleanup101 #GreatestAmericanCleanup #VolunteerTN #CleanGreenTN #nobodytrashestennessee
items.content_html:
🎥🧹 Cleanup planning just got easier.
Our new **Cleanup 101** video is now live on the Keep Tennessee Beautiful YouTube channel — packed with tips to help you organize a successful cleanup in your community.
From registering your event and requesting supplies to cleanup safety and volunteer prep, this video walks you through everything you need to know to get started.
💚 There’s still time to participate in The Greatest American Cleanup before July 4.
Watch, share, and help make a difference in your community.
#KeepTennesseeBeautiful #Cleanup101 #GreatestAmericanCleanup #VolunteerTN #CleanGreenTN #nobodytrashestennessee

items.title: ⚡ US National Lightning Safety Awareness Week | June 21–27  According to the US Centers for Disease Control and Preventi...
items.content_text:
⚡ US National Lightning Safety Awareness Week | June 21–27 
 According to the US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), people are at high risk of being struck by lightning if they work: 
 🌲 Outdoors in open spaces 
 🏭 On or near tall objects 
 💥 Around materials that conduct electricity 
 🌩️For waste and resource management professionals, a typical workday may involve all three. Fortunately, there are steps you can take to stay safe before, during, and after a lightning storm. 
 🔗Learn more at https://bit.ly/3Siqi4A  
 #SWANA #SafetyFirst #SWANASafety #LightningSafety #ResourceManagement

items.title: The final type of scam we're highlighting is law enforcement scams. In these cases, fraudsters will contact you while im...
items.content_text:
The final type of scam we're highlighting is law enforcement scams. In these cases, fraudsters will contact you while impersonating an officer, Federal agent, or bondsman. They'll claim you have an unpaid ticket, missed jury duty, have an unresolved warrant, or that you need to pay bond for a relative in jail, and then try to get you to pay them using gift cards, cryptocurrency, or digital payment services (Zelle, Apple Cash, PayPal, etc.). To be more believable, these scammers may even spoof a real law enforcement agency's phone number, tricking your caller ID.
 Here's what you need to know:
 1. No legitimate law enforcement agency will ask you to pay fees, balances, or bonds with these payment methods.
 1. No legitimate law enforcement agency will ask you to pay fees, balances, or bonds with these payment methods.
 2. If you receive one of these calls, hang up immediately.
 2. If you receive one of these calls, hang up immediately.
 3. You can call the law enforcement agency they claim to represent to verify if the call was legitimate. Make sure to look up the agency's number on their website
 3. You can call the law enforcement agency they claim to represent to verify if the call was legitimate. Make sure to look up the agency's number on their website.

items.title: Fun fact: Hamilton County was officially established on October 25, 1819! From the region’s earliest settlements to the...
items.content_text:
Fun fact: Hamilton County was officially established on October 25, 1819!
 From the region’s earliest settlements to the growth of Chattanooga, our history has shaped just what Hamilton County is today. Join us at the United250 to celebrate what makes not only our country, but our county, so special!
 🗓️ Sat, July 4, 2026
 📍 Ross's Landing - Chattanooga, TN
 💲 Free Event

items.title: Participate online in the 50th Anniversary #OECD Global Forum on Responsible Business Conduct (RBC) on 29-30 June.  The...
items.content_text:
Participate online in the 50th Anniversary #OECD Global Forum on Responsible Business Conduct (RBC) on 29-30 June. 
 The event will mark 50 years since the OECD created the first international standard on RBC. 
 It will feature the launch of two major publications: 
 ➡️ The inaugural OECD Responsible Business Outlook: a global stock-take of responsible business due diligence among the 10 000 largest globally listed companies  
 ➡️ The annual report on the activities of National Contact Points for RBC 
 Register now to join the Forum: https://brnw.ch/21x3xft
 #OECDResponsibleBusinessConduct

items.title: The Hamilton County Sheriff’s Office is investigating a single-vehicle crash with a fatality that occurred in the 600 Bl...
items.content_text:
The Hamilton County Sheriff’s Office is investigating a single-vehicle crash with a fatality that occurred in the 600 Block of Lee Pike earlier today. The driver of the vehicle was found in critical condition and was transported to the hospital via Life-Force helicopter. The passenger was pronounced dead at the scene.  
 This information is preliminary and subject to change. No further details are available.

items.title: Since Team Spain arrived in Chattanooga, it has been an incredible experience watching our community come together to ce...
items.content_text:
Since Team Spain arrived in Chattanooga, it has been an incredible experience watching our community come together to celebrate the world’s game.
 From welcoming the team to producing unforgettable watch parties along the riverfront, Hamilton County was proud to be part of creating moments that connected fans, visitors, and our community through soccer.
 A special thank you to Chattanooga Tourism Co., the City of Chattanooga, and the Tennessee Sports & Event Corporation for their partnership and hard work in bringing these events to life. Also, a very special thank you to the many First Responder agencies that supported these events and kept everyone safe. Together, we showcased the very best of Chattanooga, Hamilton County, and Tennessee.
 ⚽🇪🇸 Thank you, Team Spain, and best of luck on the rest of your World Cup journey!
  #worldcup #tennessee #HamiltonCountyTN #spain🇪🇸  #FIFAWorldCup2026

items.title: 2026 ADA Celebration Vendor Spotlight: Southeast Tennessee Area Agency on Aging and Disability  The mission of the South...
items.content_text:
2026 ADA Celebration Vendor Spotlight: Southeast Tennessee Area Agency on Aging and Disability 
 The mission of the Southeast Tennessee Area Agency on Aging and Disability is to help older adults and people with disabilities live with dignity and choices in their homes and communities. Learn more about the services they provide: https://setaaad.org/services
 Join us July 24th, 2026 from 10am to 1pm at the Walker Pavilion in Coolidge Park to celebrate the 36th anniversary of the Americans with Disabilities Act.

items.title: Just outside Chattanooga, there’s a place where history runs deep, like, really deep. Hale’s Bar Marina sits near one of...
items.content_text:
Just outside Chattanooga, there’s a place where history runs deep, like, really deep.
 Hale’s Bar Marina sits near one of the South’s oldest hydroelectric dam sites, now surrounded by quiet water, caves and stories you just have to be here to believe!
 Learn more. 👉  https://www.visitchattanooga.com/…/why-hales-bar-marina-is…/
 📸 Reese Cavitt
 #VisitChatt #Chattanooga

items.title: How can institutional investors help tackle earnings inequality? Big institutional investors such as pension funds, insu...
items.content_text:
How can institutional investors help tackle earnings inequality?
 Big institutional investors such as pension funds, insurers and asset managers invest across the economy for the long term, so they have an impact in the factors that influence employment, wage growth and economic stability.
 Join our workshop, supported by the @[urn:li:organization:2221:AXA] Foundation for Human Progress (initially AXA Research Fund), on 26 June 10:00 -12:00 CEST to discuss how to close critical data and measurement gaps and support a more effective investor engagement.
 The workshop contributes to the OECD Observatory on Social Mobility and Equal Opportunity by advancing the understanding of how the private sector can promote fairer outcomes for workers.
 Learn more and register: https://brnw.ch/21x3wGM
